title: DB schema: pipeline control tables
analysis: CatalogDB in autopilot/db.py uses direct SQL with sqlite3.Row factory, returning dict[str, object] | None for single rows and list[dict[str, object]] for multi-row queries. Schema is created in _create_schema() via executescript with CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS. The existing test suite uses a 'catalog_db' fixture from conftest.py that creates an in-memory DB with autocommit. Tests are organized in classes per table group. We need to: (1) add 4 new tables to _create_schema() with appropriate indexes, (2) implement ~18 CRUD methods following existing patterns, (3) write comprehensive tests in a new test_db_pipeline.py file. The update_activity_cluster pattern (kwargs-based SET clause with f-string) should be reused for update_gate, update_job, and update_run. The EXPECTED_TABLES list in test_db.py and the table count assertion (15) need updating to account for the 4 new tables. The _create_schema docstring says '15 catalog tables' which becomes '19 catalog tables'.

prerequisites.description: Update EXPECTED_TABLES in tests/test_db.py to include the 4 new pipeline tables (pipeline_events, pipeline_gates, pipeline_jobs, pipeline_runs — alphabetically sorted) and change the table count from 15 to 19 in test_schema_is_idempotent. Update _create_schema docstring from 15 to 19.

steps.description: Write failing test class TestPipelineSchema in test_db_pipeline.py — verify pipeline_gates, pipeline_jobs, pipeline_events, pipeline_runs tables exist with correct columns (check PRAGMA table_info for each), verify indexes exist on pipeline_jobs(stage,status), pipeline_jobs(run_id), pipeline_events(event_type), pipeline_events(created_at)

Add 4 CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS statements to _create_schema(): pipeline_gates (stage TEXT PK, mode TEXT DEFAULT 'auto', status TEXT DEFAULT 'idle', decided_at TEXT, decided_by TEXT DEFAULT 'system', notes TEXT, timeout_hours REAL), pipeline_jobs (job_id TEXT PK, stage TEXT NOT NULL, job_type TEXT NOT NULL, target_id TEXT, target_label TEXT, status TEXT DEFAULT 'pending', started_at TEXT, finished_at TEXT, duration_seconds REAL, progress_pct REAL, error_message TEXT, worker TEXT, run_id TEXT), pipeline_events (event_id INTEGER PK AUTOINCREMENT, event_type TEXT NOT NULL, stage TEXT, job_id TEXT, payload_json TEXT, created_at TEXT DEFAULT (datetime('now'))), pipeline_runs (run_id TEXT PK, started_at TEXT NOT NULL, finished_at TEXT, config_snapshot TEXT, current_stage TEXT, status TEXT DEFAULT 'running', wall_clock_seconds REAL, budget_remaining_seconds REAL). Add CREATE INDEX statements for pipeline_jobs and pipeline_events.

Write failing tests TestGatesCRUD: test_init_default_gates_populates_stages (known pipeline stages get rows), test_init_default_gates_is_idempotent (calling twice doesn't duplicate or overwrite), test_get_gate_returns_dict (returns correct dict with all fields), test_get_gate_not_found_returns_none, test_get_all_gates_returns_list, test_update_gate_modifies_fields (change mode, status, notes etc.), test_update_gate_noop_on_empty_kwargs

Implement gates CRUD on CatalogDB: init_default_gates() uses INSERT OR IGNORE for each known pipeline stage (ingest, analyze, classify, narrate, script, edl, source, render, upload), get_gate(stage) returns dict|None, get_all_gates() returns list[dict], update_gate(stage, **kwargs) uses dynamic SET clause following update_activity_cluster pattern

Write failing tests TestJobsCRUD: test_insert_job_all_fields, test_insert_job_minimal_fields (only required fields), test_get_job_returns_dict, test_get_job_not_found, test_update_job_modifies_fields, test_list_jobs_no_filter (returns all), test_list_jobs_filter_by_stage, test_list_jobs_filter_by_status, test_list_jobs_filter_by_job_type, test_list_jobs_filter_by_run_id, test_list_jobs_combined_filters, test_list_jobs_empty_result, test_count_jobs_by_status_returns_dict, test_count_jobs_by_status_with_run_id_filter

Implement jobs CRUD: insert_job(job_id, stage, job_type, **optional fields) with INSERT, get_job(job_id) returns dict|None, update_job(job_id, **kwargs) with dynamic SET, list_jobs(stage=None, status=None, job_type=None, run_id=None) builds WHERE clauses dynamically with parameter binding, count_jobs_by_status(stage, run_id=None) uses GROUP BY status returning dict[str,int]

Write failing tests TestEventsCRUD: test_insert_event_returns_event_id, test_insert_event_auto_creates_at, test_insert_event_with_all_fields, test_get_events_since_returns_newer_events, test_get_events_since_zero_returns_all, test_get_events_since_empty, test_prune_events_removes_old (insert events with old created_at, verify pruned), test_prune_events_keeps_recent

Implement events CRUD: insert_event(event_type, stage=None, job_id=None, payload_json=None) inserts and returns cursor.lastrowid as int, get_events_since(event_id) selects WHERE event_id > ? ORDER BY event_id, prune_events(hours=24) deletes WHERE created_at < datetime('now', '-N hours') using string formatting for the hours value in the SQL modifier

Write failing tests TestRunsCRUD: test_insert_run_minimal, test_insert_run_all_fields, test_get_run_returns_dict, test_get_run_not_found, test_update_run_modifies_fields, test_get_current_run_returns_latest_running, test_get_current_run_ignores_completed, test_get_current_run_returns_none_when_no_running, test_list_runs_ordered_by_started_at_desc

Implement runs CRUD: insert_run(run_id, started_at, config_snapshot=None, current_stage=None, status='running', budget_remaining_seconds=None), update_run(run_id, **kwargs) with dynamic SET, get_current_run() selects WHERE status='running' ORDER BY started_at DESC LIMIT 1, get_run(run_id) returns dict|None, list_runs() returns all ORDER BY started_at DESC

Write integration tests TestPipelineCrossTable: test_jobs_reference_run_id (insert run, insert jobs with run_id, count_jobs_by_status with run filter works), test_events_reference_job_id (insert event with job_id, retrieve and verify), test_update_nonexistent_gate_is_noop (no error, 0 rows affected), test_update_nonexistent_job_is_noop
Fix any edge cases surfaced by integration tests. Run full test suite: pytest tests/test_db_pipeline.py tests/test_db.py to verify all pass

design_decisions.decision: Use kwargs-based dynamic SET clause for update methods (update_gate, update_job, update_run)
design_decisions.rationale: Follows existing update_activity_cluster pattern in CatalogDB — callers pass only the fields they want to change, avoiding None-overwrites. Uses f-string with parameter binding for SQL safety (same as existing code with noqa: S608).

design_decisions.decision: init_default_gates() uses INSERT OR IGNORE to be idempotent
design_decisions.rationale: Pipeline stages are known at design time; INSERT OR IGNORE means calling init_default_gates() multiple times is safe and won't overwrite user-edited gate settings.

design_decisions.decision: insert_event() returns the auto-generated event_id via cursor.lastrowid
design_decisions.rationale: AUTOINCREMENT PK means caller needs the generated id for subsequent operations like get_events_since(); lastrowid is the standard sqlite3 mechanism for this.

design_decisions.decision: pipeline_events.created_at uses SQL DEFAULT (datetime('now')) rather than Python-generated timestamps
design_decisions.rationale: Keeps timestamps consistent with DB clock, simplifies prune_events() which uses SQL datetime comparison directly. For testing prune_events, we'll manually insert rows with backdated created_at.

design_decisions.decision: list_jobs() builds WHERE clause dynamically from provided filter kwargs
design_decisions.rationale: Avoids N separate query methods; single method handles all filter combinations with parameter binding for SQL safety. Matches the pattern of optional filter in list_narratives().

design_decisions.decision: count_jobs_by_status() returns dict[str, int] mapping status to count
design_decisions.rationale: More useful than raw rows for dashboard display — caller can immediately check counts.get('running', 0), counts.get('error', 0) etc.

design_decisions.decision: New test file tests/test_db_pipeline.py rather than appending to test_db.py
design_decisions.rationale: Task spec explicitly requests this file. Keeps pipeline control tests separate from existing catalog tests, enables concurrent development.

design_decisions.decision: Update EXPECTED_TABLES and table count in test_db.py as a prerequisite
design_decisions.rationale: Existing schema tests (test_schema_creates_all_13_tables, test_schema_is_idempotent) will fail when new tables are added. Must update them to expect 19 tables before adding schema.

reuse.how: Reuse directly in test_db_pipeline.py — all new pipeline tests use this fixture for isolated in-memory DB instances
Copy pattern for update_gate(), update_job(), update_run() — build SET clause from kwargs keys with f-string, append PK to values list, use parameter binding

reuse.where: autopilot/db.py throughout (e.g. get_media line 260, list_narratives line 643)
reuse.how: All query methods return dict(row) for single rows or [dict(row) for row in cur.fetchall()] for multiple rows — apply to all new methods

reuse.what: _create_schema() executescript pattern with CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS
reuse.how: Append new CREATE TABLE and CREATE INDEX statements to the existing executescript string
Reference for list_jobs() — demonstrates optional WHERE clause with parameter binding when filter is not None
